title: Spike: dependency upgrades and Python 3.14 compatibility
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

## Context
- pyproject.toml pins python >=3.12,<4
- .python-version pins 3.13
- Devcontainer installs python 3.13 via feature
- Local mise resolved to 3.14.4 (user's machine)
- Backend is running and passing tests on 3.14 locally, but compatibility is unverified

## Questions to Answer
1. Do all dependencies (sqlmodel, fastapi, openai, langchain, etc.) support Python 3.14?
2. Are there any 3.14-specific deprecation warnings or breaking changes in our deps?
3. Should we pin to 3.13 for stability or adopt 3.14?

## Tasks
- [ ] Run uv pip audit / dependency review for outdated packages
- [ ] Run full test suite (1327 tests) on Python 3.14 and note any failures
- [ ] Run full test suite on Python 3.13 as baseline comparison
- [ ] Check pydantic v2, sqlmodel, openai SDK, fastapi changelogs for 3.14 support
- [ ] Update .python-version and devcontainer if adopting 3.14
- [ ] Update pyproject.toml requires-python if pinning to 3.13

## Recommendation
If 3.14 is clean, adopt it (performance improvements, free-threading opt-in later). If any deps fail, pin to 3.13 and revisit next quarter.
